openingMessage: In leadership, there are no words more important than trust.
leadershipThought: You are not just doing a job. You are building the next generation of people who will do this safely. That is what it means to be an Owl.

fieldNews.headline: Raymundo Solis: Had to take a break and wait for chemical tote to be put back before reconnecting the well
Jordan Lemmons: Casing valve is leaking by after they blew down well so stopped job to get it greased — Let me know it was leaking and what the plan was to bring someone out to grease
Cindy Smith: Crew did a conversion. No gas was detected. — All safety measures were implemented
Baldemar Bracamontes: We had a different crew doing electrical work on a well next to us, we made our LSRAs — The disconnect got done quickly and safely before the rig got to location
Stefanie Kirschke: Safe execution of well conversion. Air test completed and bleeders verified prior to work. — Crew followed procedures verified zero energy and maintained good communication
SM Energy #112 — 6,000 ppm H2S / 2% CO2 (carry-forward from 7/10)
fieldNews.detail: Michael Hutt reported it Friday. Confirm current readings before any entry or first break this week.

fieldNews.headline: Two driving near-misses flagged Friday — stay heads-up on the road
fieldNews.detail: Jordan Lemmons (swerving driver, Jal 128) and Alex Clubb (service truck into his lane at 75 mph). The drive is part of the job.

todaysChallenge: At your next location, think about one thing you would do differently if a client were standing next to you.
todaysChallengeSubtext: If you'd change your behavior because someone was watching — that's your standard. Hold it whether they are or not.

compliance.footerNote: Week 4 final — update with Week 5 counts Monday AM
